mission.title: Install and Configure Weaviate
mission.description: Weaviate is an open-source vector database designed for semantic search and data retrieval, combining vector similarity search with structured filtering. It is ideal for applications requiring scalable and fault-tolerant data storage, such as recommendation engines and chatbots. Key trade-offs include the complexity of setup and the need for proper resource allocation in production environments.

steps.title: Add Weaviate Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the Weaviate Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration, allowing you to install Weaviate using Helm.

```bash

helm repo add weaviate https://weaviate.io/helm-charts

helm repo update

```

> This command adds the Weaviate Helm repository.
> Updates the Helm repository to ensure you have the latest charts.

steps.title: Install Weaviate CRDs
steps.description:
Weaviate requires Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) to be installed before deploying the main application.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/weaviate/weaviate/main/deploy/crds/weaviate.io_weaviates.yaml

```

> This command installs the necessary CRDs for Weaviate.

steps.title: Install Weaviate using Helm
steps.description:
This command installs Weaviate in the specified namespace with the desired version.

```bash

helm install weaviate weaviate/weaviate --namespace weaviate --create-namespace --version 1.36.0

```

> Installs Weaviate in the 'weaviate' namespace.

steps.title: Create Weaviate instance
steps.description:
This step creates a Weaviate instance by applying a custom resource definition (CR) that defines the configuration for your Weaviate deployment.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: weaviate.io/v1
kind: Weaviate
metadata:
  name: weaviate-instance
  namespace: weaviate
spec:
  replication:
    enabled: true
EOF

```

> Creates a Weaviate instance with replication enabled.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: weaviate.io/v1
kind: Weaviate
metadata:
  name: weaviate-instance
  namespace: weaviate
spec:
  replication:
    enabled: true
EOF

steps.title: Verify Weaviate is running
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the Weaviate deployment to ensure it is running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n weaviate

```

> Lists all pods in the Weaviate namespace to verify the deployment.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Weaviate
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Weaviate deployment and associated resources. Be aware that this will result in data loss if persistent storage is not configured.

```bash

helm uninstall weaviate --namespace weaviate

kubectl delete namespace weaviate

```

> Uninstalls the Weaviate release.
> Deletes the Weaviate namespace and all associated resources.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Weaviate
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up your data. This process includes draining the pods, upgrading the Helm chart, and verifying the upgrade.

```bash

kubectl drain <pod-name> --ignore-daemonsets

helm upgrade weaviate weaviate/weaviate --namespace weaviate --version 1.36.0

kubectl get pods -n weaviate

```

> Drains the pod to prepare for upgrade.
> Upgrades Weaviate to the specified version.
> Verifies that the upgraded pods are running.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Weaviate is not running'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Weaviate pods may not have started correctly due to resource constraints.

**Fix:**
Check the pod logs with 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n weaviate' to diagnose the issue.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Connection refused'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Weaviate service may not be exposed correctly.

**Fix:**
Ensure the service is created and check with 'kubectl get services -n weaviate'.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'CRD not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Custom Resource Definitions for Weaviate may not have been applied.

**Fix:**
Reapply the CRDs using 'kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/weaviate/weaviate/main/deploy/crds/weaviate.io_weaviates.yaml'.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient resources'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The cluster may not have enough resources allocated for Weaviate.

**Fix:**
Increase the resource limits in the Weaviate configuration and redeploy.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new vectorization models and improved query performance.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated the old vectorization API endpoints.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v1.35.x, ensure to update your API calls to the new endpoints.

resolution.summary: You should see the Weaviate pods running with a status of 'Running' when executing 'kubectl get pods -n weaviate'.

spec:

    enabled: true
EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with sufficient resources and the necessary tools installed.
